title: Fix v2s1 parsing, and use c/image for config conversion.
body:
<s>DO NOT MERGE YET, this includes #775, which needs to be reviewed first.</s>

---

Don't ignore v2s1 history if `docker_version` is not set:

`buildah` itself clears `docker_version` in `Builder.fixupConfig`; so, buildah-created v2s1 images do not pass this test, `buildah from buildah-created-v2s1` creates an image with empty history, which causes `buildah commit` to complain about a mismatch between the history and layer `DiffID` list.

Now also includes a fair amount of subsequent cleanups.

---

<s>WIP, this one-liner is the ultimate cause, but really the first problem is the extremely forgiving error handling which caused this failure to be silently ignored.  I should also check whether the conversion code in here needs to exist at all, or whether c/image could be used to get the data.</s>

comments.body:
OK, this is now ready for review.  I have made this separate from #775 ; both are necessary to fix #684, but the two PRs are conceptually completely independent.

This PR has 3 parts; I can split them out if that would be preferable:
- Immediate fix: don’t expect `docker_version` to exist in v2s1 images.
- A series of cleanups and refactorings, primarily making the error handling stricter, also eliminating some duplication.
- A final commit which wholesale replaces the single-user config conversion implementations with using the conversion in `containers/image`; depends on the previous refactorings to make information available.

See the individual commit messages for more details.

LGTM
@nalind We need you to review this.
bot, retest this please
@giuseppe @umohnani8 PTAL
LGTM
@pixdrift Could you test this out also.

I'll try on a new VM in case I messed something up, but pulling this commit and building from it, I'm getting a new and different different error:

```
# Setup a private registry
docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --name registry registry:latest

# Pull a container image from docker.io and push it to private registry
newcontainer=$(buildah from alpine)
buildah commit ${newcontainer} myalpine
buildah push --format v2s1 --tls-verify=false myalpine docker://localhost:5000/alpinev2s1

# Use buildah to pull from the private registry and inspect the result to see broken metadata
brokencontainer=$(buildah from --tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5000/alpinev2s1)
buildah inspect ${brokencontainer} | grep Manifest

# Generate error by attempting to commit image
# buildah commit ${brokencontainer} broken-layers
error committing container "alpinev2s1-working-container" to "broken-layers": error copying layers and metadata: Error initializing source containers-storage:alpinev2s1-working-container: internal error: history lists 3 non-empty layers, but we have 4 layers on disk

```

> @pixdrift Could you test this out also.

@pixdrift @TomSweeneyRedHat Note that this is not a stand-alone fix: _both_ this and #775 are necessary for the reproducer which involves `buildah push`.


> @mtrmac, my bad, I thought you'd added #775 to this.

I did originally, but then took it out. I apologize for the confusion.

The test that @pixdrift passed with flying colors, but the tests/test_buildah_baseline.sh script failed.  I'm not quit sure what's going on, but I've been able to create a small reproducer.  Will test more in the morning:

```
# newcontainer=$(buildah from scratch)
# newcontainer=$(buildah from scratch)
# echo $newcontainer
working-container-2
# buildah containers
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
d2f31008507d     *                  scratch                          working-container
91ef873b25eb     *                  scratch                          working-container-2 

# scratchmnt=$(buildah mount $newcontainer)
error reading build container "working-container-2": error reading build container: container not known

Same error if I try to do commands like:
buildah copy $newcontainer $FILE /usr/bin
buildah config --cmd /usr/bin/runecho.sh $newcontainer
```
This doesn't seem to be particular to scratch images, same issue if I do `buildah from alpine`.  It seems that the second container with the '-2' on it is not findable for some reason.  I can remove it if I do `buildah rm -a` but not `buildah rm working-container-2`

@mtrmac 

I know what the problem is, still trying to find the solution.  If I print out the containerStore.byname map, in ./vendor/github.com/containers/storage/containers.go I'm finding:

```
map[alpine-working-container:0xc4202cc340 alpine-working-container-2 :0xc4202cc410]}]
```

The second and beyond container that's created has some kind of rogue character after it's name.  In the Load() function I tried to do a strings.TrimSpace(name) just before this:

```
                               if conflict, ok := names[name]; ok {
                                        r.removeName(conflict, name)
                                        needSave = true
                                }
                                names[name] = containers[n]
```

But the character isn't being removed.  So I added:

```
name = name[:len(name)-1]
``` 

and now I can remove that container using it's name.

But that doesn't make me feel safe and actually messes up the first entry without the rogue character.  Will look tomorrow to see if I can figure out where that's coming from but thought I'd post my findings in case it stirs anything with you or others.
Forgot to mention in my previous comment that the extra trailing character is not in the name if I clone the latest master from GitHub.  So I'm pretty sure it's somewhere in these changes.

@TomSweeneyRedHat What branch and OS is this happening on? With F28, I can reproduce this neither with `scratch` nor `alpine`; neither on a checkout of this PR + #775 as is, nor on the two + current `master` (fc438bb932e891cbe04109cfae1dfbe3c99307a5), e.g.:

```console
# ./buildah rm -a
# ./buildah rmi -a
…
# ./buildah from scratch
working-container
# a=$(./buildah from scratch)
# ./buildah mount "$a"
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/83c680f05b861bcb1807ef85ca559d649f54f1c33edb8cc8c29551244cf5ca54/merged
# ./buildah from alpine
…
alpine-working-container
# b=$(./buildah from alpine)
# ./buildah mount "$b"
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/80e38e83f03bcbc74d72fd48dbe8fa962dc15b6701f0b5f62ecfcdcacd12c685/merged
# echo $a $b
working-container-2 alpine-working-container-2
```

@mtrmac I did this testing on a newly installed F28 and did 'dnf -y update'.  I then cloned Buildah from GitHub and then did:

```
git checkout -b mtrmac-v2s1-parsing master
git pull https://github.com/mtrmac/buildah.git v2s1-parsing
```

I then took the files from #775 and replaced them in my sandbox, then did make and make install.

I'll go set up a second VM from scratch and will try again.  That would be a kick in the head if I messed up some bit somewhere that's causing this issue.

Well CRUD!  Apparently I kicked myself in the head.  I just set up  a fresh test system, reran the tests and everything works fine, both the baseline and the original tests from the issue pass with flying colors.  Sorry for the fire drill @mtrmac and all, but I'm completely puzzled how the first systemd got messed up.

Would like @pixdrift to test/review too and a review from @nalind would be awesome.
